statement: The acquired 1949 inquest/victimology record treats the subject as the unknown man found at Somerton; Somerton Man and Tamam Shud man are project labels.
Pre-death birth, family, residence, employment, education, military service, finances, and associates are not verified for the deceased in acquired primary records.
The Webb attribution is a serious researcher/lab-led identification lead, but no final coroner/SAPOL/FSSA report is acquired.
Dwyer described the body as tallish, about 45 years, and in good physical condition; exact measurements are not precisely established in extracted pages.
Hair is greying; eye colour is not established; cared-for nails/feet and scars/marks are observational identifiers only.
Dental chart exists and several teeth were missing, but exact notation needs specialist/manual transcription.
confidence: High for existence; Moderate for notation

statement: Fingerprints were taken and circulated; chart reproduction exists, but original chart and full comparison logs are incomplete.
Rail and bus tickets are high-confidence property records but do not identify purchaser/holder or complete route.
confidence: High for records; unknown for holder

statement: Movement reconstruction includes 11 records from ticket issue through postmortem and suitcase recovery.
Relationship map contains 18 entries; 14 are official or witness contacts after death rather than personal relationships.
No verified family, friend, employer, or pre-death associate relationship is established in acquired primary archive.
Jessica Thomson connection is partially verified/unable to verify and not sufficient to establish personal relationship with deceased; Robin Thomson is not established here.
Alf Boxall is a later Rubaiyat-related lead, not a verified direct relationship to the deceased.
Historic identification attempts included fingerprints, photographs, dental charting, circulation, and inquiry, but acquired 1949 record remained unidentified.
Mission 11 records Abbott's July 2022 Webb announcement, DNA from plaster-bust hairs, Astrea rootless-hair DNA account, and Fitzpatrick genealogy method.
Mission 11 records ABC civil-record details for Carl Webb and a 5 Oct 1951 Trove legal notice; use remains conditional on official identity confirmation.
ABC 6 July 2026 reporting says SAPOL had not yet presented findings to coroner; Mission 11 located no peer-reviewed public DNA paper or official coroner finding.
Controlled identity wording is researcher/lab-led Carl Charles Webb attribution pending official coroner or South Australia Police determination.
Strongest victimology facts are post-discovery and source-mapped: unidentified male, property, tickets, witnesses, fingerprints, dental charting, photographs, death mask/bust, specimens.

Largest unknowns include pre-death biography, official identity status, exact movements, personal relationships, residence, employment, finances, purpose at Somerton, and evening-sighting identity continuity.
